page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the n4 family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical Intel x86_64 cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The Intel processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, AVX512_BF16). Cryptographic acceleration is supported via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI flags, alongside integer and bit manipulation extensions like BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong performance in memory-intensive and multi-threaded operations. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 254.09 GB/sec for read operations and 113.1 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core performance is robust, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 16867.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 58640.65. The hardware-accelerated matrix instructions contribute to strong prompt processing speeds in LLM inference, achieving 2234.41 tokens/sec on small models and 179.15 tokens/sec on medium models.

Given its high memory density and dedicated compute resources, the n4-highmem-48 offers a balanced qualitative cost-to-resource profile for memory-bound enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for database operations, as evidenced by a strong PassMark database score of 24009.26, as well as static web serving, text processing, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines. The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel x86_64 architecture, it features 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores and 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a high 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. Benchmark results show strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth, multi-core processing, and database operations, alongside efficient LLM prompt processing. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is optimized for memory-bound applications. It is highly suited for database management, static web serving, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines where high memory-to-CPU ratios are required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and advanced AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, delivering strong database and multi-core performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-48 is a memory-optimized server with 48 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, built for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server with forty-eight dedicated vCPUs, three hundred eighty-four gigabytes of RAM, and advanced matrix math acceleration.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.
Intel x86_64 architecture with Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 support.
Strong large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities.
Optimized for database operations, static web serving, and memory-bound workloads.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the default configuration.
